result:
Done. The change request is implemented, tested, and committed.

## What I built

The sandbox was a fresh checkout with only scaffolding (`package.json`, `README.md`), so I built a minimal dependency-free Node HTTP app around the requested feature, matching the repo's setup (ESM, `node --test`, no external deps):

- **`src/store.js`** — in-memory users + sessions, scrypt-hashed passwords. Crucially, **sessions store only a user id**, never the role.
- **`src/app.js`** — the HTTP router with the two new endpoints plus `POST /login`.
- **`src/seed.js`** / **`src/server.js`** — seed fixture and runnable server.
- **`test/roles.test.js`** — 12 tests, each on its own freshly-seeded server.

## How each requirement is met

- **`GET /users` (admin only)** → returns `{ id, name, email, role }` per user; password hash never leaks. `401` unauthenticated, `403` non-admin.
- **`PATCH /users/:id/role` (admin only)** → accepts `{"role":"admin"|"member"}`; `400` on invalid role, `404` unknown user.
- **Takes effect immediately for signed-in users** → authorization looks the user (and their current role) up from the store on *every* request, so a live session gains/loses admin access on its very next call with no re-login. Two tests assert this directly (live promotion and live demotion).
- **Last remaining admin can't be demoted** → guarded with a live admin count, returning `409`; a companion test confirms an admin *can* be demoted once a second admin exists.

All 12 tests pass and `npm run typecheck`/`lint` (node --check) is clean. Committed as `3fbbb92` on `main`.
